title: ssh-tunneling
html:
ssh-tunneling

> Built-in pivoting through any SSH access. No tools to upload. Use when you have SSH creds on a dual-homed host.

Local Port Forward

Forward a remote port to your local machine:

# Access internal_host:80 via localhost:8080
ssh -L 8080:internal_host:80 user@pivot

# Access MSSQL on internal host
ssh -L 1433:10.10.10.5:1433 user@pivot

Remote Port Forward

Expose your local port on the remote host:

# Make your port 9001 accessible on pivot:9001
ssh -R 9001:127.0.0.1:9001 user@pivot

Dynamic SOCKS Proxy

ssh -D 1080 user@pivot
# Then use proxychains with socks5 127.0.0.1 1080

Jump Hosts

ssh -J user@jump user@target
ssh -J user1@jump1,user2@jump2 user@target

ProxyCommand (persistent config)

# ~/.ssh/config
Host internal
    HostName <target-ip>
    User admin
    ProxyJump user@pivot

Tips

- Add -N to not open a shell (just tunnel) - Add -f to background the SSH process - Use autossh for persistent tunnels
